title: aglaia sepalina
intro: Aglaia sepalina, also known as the Malabar aglaia, is a plant species in the family Meliaceae, native to the tropical regions of Asia and Australia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Aglaia sepalina include reducing fever, treating respiratory infections, and alleviating pain, as well as its potential role in managing diabetes and certain types of cancer. Aglaia sepalina can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, and its extracts have also been studied for their potential use in modern medicine.
